reasoning: The Lean statement captures the informal claim exactly. The hypotheses `hp : Odd p` and `hp' : p.Prime` encode "p is an odd prime". Taking `a : (ZMod p)ˣ` is the natural encoding of "a is an integer being considered as an element of (Z/pZ)*", and `IsPrimitiveRoot (a : ZMod p) (p - 1)` is logically equivalent to "a has order p-1 in (Z/pZ)*" (i.e., a is a primitive root mod p), since `p - 1 ≥ 2` under these hypotheses. The right-hand side `∀ q : ℕ, q ∣ (p - 1) → q.Prime → ¬ (a^((p-1)/q) = 1)` precisely states "for every prime divisor q of p-1, a^((p-1)/q) ≠ 1 (mod p)". Both directions of the iff correspond to standard arguments in elementary number theory, and no hypothesis is added or dropped silently.
